channel.title: NYT > World > Middle East
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Families of Iranian Children Killed in School Airstrike Pen Letter to Pope
item.description: Pope Leo said he has seen the letter from the parents of more than 100 children killed in the strike, which a preliminary inquiry found resulted from a mistake by the U.S. military.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A memorial in Tehran for the victims of an airstrike on an elementary school in Minab, Iran, on the first day of U.S. and Israeli bombing of the country.

item.title: Trump Cancels Witkoff and Kushner’s Trip for Iran Talks, Saying, ‘We Have All the Cards.’
item.description: The last-minute scuttling of the trip to Pakistan was the latest sign of how far apart the two sides are on reaching a deal to end the Iran war.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater and Pranav Baskar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump at the White House on Thursday. He decided on Saturday not to have two of his top negotiators travel to Pakistan.

item.title: Lebanese Ask, ‘What Cease-Fire?’ as Israel and Hezbollah Trade Attacks in the South
item.description: The fragile truce in Lebanon was extended after talks in Washington this week but has come under threat as Israel and Hezbollah have traded attacks.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir
item.media:credit: David Guttenfelder/The New York Times
item.media:description: A mass funeral in Ramadiyeh, Lebanon, on Friday, mourning those killed in recent weeks in the war with Israel.

item.title: Trump Seeks to Abolish Iran’s Nuclear Stockpile, a Problem He Helped Create
item.description: President Trump withdrew from the Obama-era nuclear accord in 2018, saying it was the worst deal ever. But Iran responded with an enrichment spree that haunts the negotiations to this day.
item.dc:creator: William J. Broad and David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Arms Control and Limitation and Disarmament
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: In February in preparation for a possible war with the United States, Iran moved missile launchers into positions within striking distance of Israeli and American military forces.

item.title: Under Trump, Green Card Seekers Face New Scrutiny for Views on Israel
item.description: In guidance to immigration officers, the administration describes participating in pro-Palestinian protests and criticizing Israel as “overwhelmingly negative” factors.
item.dc:creator: Hamed Aleaziz and Nicholas Nehamas
item.media:credit: Vincent Alban/The New York Times
item.media:description: Supporters of Palestine demonstrating in New York last year. Under new guidance issued by the Trump administration, immigrants could be denied a green card for participating in pro-Palestinian protests.

item.title: Raqqa Showcases Syria’s Tumultuous Past
item.description: The city on the Euphrates, once a cradle of civilization, suffered under a succession of recent rulers. Our visit found it hoping for change.
item.dc:creator: Carlotta Gall, Hussam Hammoud and Nanna Heitmann
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
Islamic State in Iraq and Syria (ISIS)
item.media:description: Dancing in Naim Square, where ISIS once held public executions, in January after Syrian government forces captured Raqqa.

item.title: On Iran’s Border, Cooking Oil Trade Is a Snapshot of the Country’s Struggles
item.description: The war has exacerbated Iran’s economic crisis, forcing many to cross the border into Turkey to buy the most basic goods.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Kiana Hayeri for The New York Times
item.media:description: Iranians carrying cooking oil on Wednesday at the Kapikoy border crossing between Turkey and Iran.

item.title: Rubio’s Absence From Iran Talks Highlights Stay-at-Home Role
item.description: President Trump has outsourced much of his diplomacy to others, while Mr. Rubio focuses on his second job as national security adviser.
item.dc:creator: Michael Crowley
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: As President Trump prepares to send a delegation to the latest round of U.S.-Iran talks in Pakistan, his secretary of state, Marco Rubio, will remain where he often does: at home.

item.title: Kushner and Witkoff Traveling to Pakistan to Resume Iran Talks
item.description: Iran’s foreign minister has already arrived in the country, state media reported. He was believed to be carrying a written response to a U.S. proposal to end the war.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater, Farnaz Fassihi and Euan Ward
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Tehran on Thursday. Iran’s foreign minister was expected to meet with Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner in Pakistan this weekend.

item.title: U.S. Unveils More Sanctions Targeting Iran’s Shadow Fleet
item.description: The Treasury Department also targeted a top independent Chinese refinery that it said was a major buyer of Iranian petroleum.
item.dc:creator: Alan Rappeport
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Fatemeh Bahrami/Anadolu Agency, via Getty Images
item.media:description: Kharg Island, Iran’s main hub for oil exports, in 2017. The Trump administration broadened its effort to squeeze Iran’s oil industry on Friday with a range of sanctions.

item.title: Venice Biennale Bars Awards for Countries Facing Crimes Against Humanity Charges
item.description: The jury said it would “refrain from considering” countries whose leaders are facing charges of crimes against humanity, which would affect Israel and Russia.
item.dc:creator: Remy Tumin
item.category: War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
item.media:credit: Antonio Calanni/Associated Press
item.media:description: In announcing its decision to not give awards to artists from countries facing charges of crimes against humanity, the jury of the Venice Biennale said it was committed to “the defense of human rights.”

item.title: Israel-Lebanon Cease-Fire Tested by Escalating Clashes
item.description: The U.S.-mediated cease-fire halted an all-out war between Israel and Hezbollah, but their intensifying attacks on each other could put the truce at risk.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward and Sarah Chaayto
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: A disabled ambulance sat amid rubble from an Israeli strike on Nabatieh, Lebanon, earlier this week. Strikes by both Israel and Hezbollah diminished since a cease-fire began last week, but clashes have intensified in recent days.

item.title: Iran and U.S. Blockades in the Strait of Hormuz: What to Know
item.description: The foes are trying to exert control over the narrow passageway. Most ships aren’t moving. Here’s what to know.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Reuters
item.media:description: Vessels in the Strait of Hormuz on Wednesday in Musandam, Oman.

item.title: Netanyahu Reveals He Had Prostate Cancer and Underwent Radiation Therapy
item.description: The Israeli leader had surgery in 2024 for an enlarged prostate. More recently, follow-up care detected a tumor, his office disclosed on Friday.
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger
item.media:credit: Avishag Shaar-Yashuv for The New York Times
item.media:description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel delivering a speech in January. He said on Friday that he had delayed telling Israelis about his cancer for “two months.”

item.title: Trump Extends Waiver Allowing Foreign Ships to Move Goods Between U.S. Ports
item.description: The move was intended to ensure the flow of fuel in the United States, but some economists say it might reduce gas prices for consumers only a small amount.
item.dc:creator: Peter Eavis
item.media:credit: Brian Snyder/Reuters
item.media:description: A Portuguese tanker docked in Braintree, Mass., last month, after President Trump waived the Jones Act to allow foreign-flagged vessels to transport goods between American ports.

item.title: Gaza Set to Hold First Local Election in Two Decades
item.description: Hamas said it was not participating in the municipal elections in Deir al-Balah, set for this weekend. Residents said it was a long-awaited opportunity to address the city’s problems
item.dc:creator: Adam Rasgon, Bilal Shbair, Abu Bakr Bashir, Iyad Abuheweila and Saher Alghorra
item.media:credit: Saher Alghorra for The New York Times

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater, Helene Cooper, John Ismay, Farnaz Fassihi and Max Bearak

item.title: Rubio Says Iran’s Soccer Team Can Attend World Cup
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio said that while the players were welcome, anyone accompanying them who has links to the Iranian military would be denied entry.
item.dc:creator: Qasim Nauman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump and Secretary of State Marco Rubio at the White House on Thursday.

item.title: Iran War Has Drained U.S. Supplies of Critical, Costly Weapons
item.description: The Pentagon’s rush to rearm its Mideast forces makes it less ready to confront potential adversaries like Russia and China, administration and congressional officials say.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt and Jonathan Swan
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: The remains of a university building in Tehran. Two independent groups say the U.S. expense of the war in Iran so far is between $28 billion and $35 billion.

item.title: How the Iran War Is Morphing Into a Volatile Standoff in the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The conflict has morphed into a volatile standoff in the Strait of Hormuz, as the economic costs mount and President Trump faces a political backlash at home.
item.dc:creator: Anton Troianovski
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A mural in Tehran illustrates Iran’s grip on the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Trump Keeps Talking About Iran’s ‘Nuclear Dust.’ What Is It?
item.description: First of all, it isn’t dust. It’s Iran’s stockpile of near-bomb-grade uranium, which is stored in large canisters about the size of scuba tanks.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater and David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Planet Labs, via Reuters
item.media:description: A satellite image from February showing construction on buildings at the Isfahan nuclear site in Iran that were destroyed during U.S. airstrikes last June.

item.title: Lebanese Journalist Killed in Israeli Strike Was Known for Being Intrepid
item.description: Mourners paid respects to Amal Khalil, who remained trapped under rubble for hours before emergency medics recovered her body.
item.dc:creator: Max Bearak and Daniel Berehulak
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Daniel Berehulak/The New York Times
item.media:description: Family members of Amal Khalil at her funeral in Baysariyeh, Lebanon, on Thursday.

item.title: Journalist Detained in Kuwait After Posting About Iran War Is Acquitted
item.description: Ahmed Shihab-Eldin, a Kuwaiti-American journalist, is expected to be released soon, after 52 days in detention, a lawyer for two of his family members said.
item.dc:creator: Ismaeel Naar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Tristan Fewings/Getty Images
item.media:description: Ahmed Shihab‑Eldin at a film festival in Doha, Qatar, last year.

item.title: World’s Top Producer of Condoms Raises Prices as Iran War Rattles Supply Chains
item.description: The Malaysian company Karex, which produces about five billion condoms a year, said it was raising prices by 30 percent because of higher raw material prices and global shipping disruptions.
item.dc:creator: Zunaira Saieed
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Samsul Said/Bloomberg
item.media:description: Karex, a condom maker, said the conflict in Iran is causing an increase in production costs.

item.title: Zelensky details deals Ukraine signed to bolster Gulf nations’ defense against Iranian drones.
item.dc:creator: Maria Varenikova
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Yuriy Dyachyshyn/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: President Volodymyr Zelensky of Ukraine said that Ukraine had signed agreements to cooperate with Saudi Arabia, Qatar and the United Arab Emirates on technology to intercept attack drones.

item.title: How People in Lebanon Are Reacting to Talks With Israel
item.description: The Lebanese are deeply divided over whether rare direct talks with Israel are a good thing.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: David Guttenfelder/The New York Times
item.media:description: A funeral in southern Lebanon on Wednesday for people killed during the war between Hezbollah and Israel.

item.title: U.S. Forces Board a 2nd Tanker They Say Is Carrying Oil From Iran
item.description: It was the second such action this week, as the Trump administration ramps up pressure on Tehran.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: via Defense Department
item.media:description: A photo released by the Defense Department shows the M/T Majestic X. The Pentagon said U.S. forces boarded the vessel because it was carrying Iranian oil.

item.title: The Generals Who Are Now Running Iran
item.description: The killing of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei ushered in a new form of collective leadership in the country, with more power for the Revolutionary Guards.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Iranians gathered last month in Tehran with flags and posters to show their support for Mojtaba Khamenei, the new supreme leader.

item.title: Why Diesel Has Become a Much Bigger Economic Problem Than Gasoline
item.description: The war in Iran has disrupted supplies of diesel, used to power trucks and heavy equipment, much more than gasoline, which is primarily used in passenger cars.
item.dc:creator: Emmett Lindner
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas
item.media:credit: Adam Amengual for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Port of Los Angeles. Large semi trucks and some ships run on diesel fuel.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater, Euan Ward, Max Bearak and Chris Cameron

item.title: A Lost Tribe in India Makes Its Exodus to Israel
item.description: The B’nei Menashe believe they are the descendants of Manasseh, a tribal patriarch from thousands of years ago. Evidence is thin, but Israel is welcoming them “home.”
item.dc:creator: Hari Kumar, Alex Travelli and Atul Loke
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: People from B’nei Menashe community pray at a synagogue in the kibbutz near Churachandpur, in India’s Manipur state, in March.

item.title: Israeli Strikes Kill a Journalist and Injure Another in Lebanon
item.description: Lebanese officials say rescue teams came under fire while trying to aid Amal Khalil, a reporter for a Lebanese newspaper, and Zeinab Faraj, a photojournalist.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Mohammed Zaatari/Associated Press
item.media:description: Amal Khalil, who worked for Al-Akhbar, reporting near a destroyed bridge last month. She was killed on Wednesday.

item.title: Iran Says It Seized Two Ships Near the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: A British naval agency said the two vessels had come under attack near the strait, as a U.S. blockade on Iran’s ports continued.
item.dc:creator: Lynsey Chutel, Leo Sands and Michael Levenson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A poster of the new supreme leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, in Tehran on Tuesday.

item.title: With peace talks postponed, Pakistanis feel the cost of the war in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Elian Peltier
item.media:credit: Elian Peltier/The New York Times
item.media:description: A banner in Rawalpindi, Pakistan, celebrates the country’s efforts to mediate between the United States and Iran.

item.title: How the War Powers Act Could Pressure Trump to End the Iran War
item.description: A decades-old law allows the president to wage war without congressional approval for 60 days, then limits his options for continuing. President Trump may seek to get around it.
item.dc:creator: Robert Jimison
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Henry Nicholls/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Loading bombs onto a U.S. Air Force B-1 bomber at an air base in Britain last month.

item.title: US Withholding Iraq’s Own Oil Money Over Ties to Iran
item.description: The United States has halted dollar shipments to Iraq’s cash-based economy in an effort to force the Baghdad government to distance itself from Iran. The money comes from Iraq’s oil revenues.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon and Raja Abdulrahim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Federal Reserve Bank of New York
item.media:credit: Ahmad Al-Rubaye/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Members of Iraq’s Popular Mobilization Forces, an umbrella group of militias, during a funeral in Baghdad this month.

item.title: Bessent Backs Financial Support for Oil-Rich U.A.E.
item.description: The Treasury secretary said that currency swap line would benefit both the United Arab Emirates and the United States.
item.dc:creator: Alan Rappeport
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
item.media:description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent testified on Capitol Hill on Wednesday.

item.title: Whether for or Against the War, Iranians Say They’re Feeling Economic Pain
item.description: At a border crossing in Turkey, one Iranian woman said her friends struggled to afford food because there was no work to be found and strikes had forced factories to close.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Kiana Hayeri for The New York Times
item.media:description: A family saying goodbye to their relatives who are leaving Iran, at the Kapikoy border crossing in Turkey on Wednesday.

item.title: On Independence Day, Israel Honors Rabbi Who Called to ‘Flatten’ Gaza
item.description: Avraham Zarbiv became known on Israel’s right for demolishing buildings in Gaza. He lit a torch at a national ceremony on Tuesday evening.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman
item.media:credit: Saher Alghorra for The New York Times
item.media:description: Palestinians returning to their homes in northern Gaza in October 2025 after Israel and Hamas agreed on a prisoner exchange deal and a cease-fire.

item.title: Attacks along the Israel-Lebanon border put a cease-fire under strain.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward
item.media:credit: David Guttenfelder/The New York Times
item.media:description: A family drove through the ruins of Mansour, a village in southern Lebanon, on Tuesday.

item.title: Strait of Hormuz Dangers Escalate as Iran Says It Seized 2 Cargo Ships
item.description: Hours after President Trump extended a cease-fire, Tehran appeared to be exerting its authority in the contested waters near the strait.
item.dc:creator: Lynsey Chutel and Leo Sands
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Reuters
item.media:description: In the Strait of Hormuz off Musandam, Oman, on Wednesday.

item.title: To Iran, Trump Blinked First by Extending the Cease-Fire
item.description: Iran’s leaders believe that they can withstand an enduring standoff longer than President Trump. The strategy could be economically devastating for average Iranians.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: An Iran expert said the major impediment to negotiations restarting was the same as it was before talks — both countries see themselves as having the advantage.

item.title: Iranian Expats in U.A.E. Caught Between Home and Homeland
item.description: Iran’s retaliatory attacks on the United Arab Emirates have put the U.A.E.’s large Iranian expatriate community in an uncomfortable position.
item.dc:creator: Raja Abdulrahim and Natalie Naccache
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Natalie Naccache for The New York Times

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Lynsey Chutel, Leo Sands, Chris Cameron and Max Bearak

item.title: What Was the 2015 Iran Nuclear Deal?
item.description: President Trump has ridiculed President Barack Obama and the agreement, which he withdrew from during his first term. His critics say he could have avoided a war had he left it in place.
item.dc:creator: Michael Crowley
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Arms Control and Limitation and Disarmament
item.media:credit: Joe Klamar/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Negotiators from the United States, Iran and European countries meeting to discuss a nuclear deal in Vienna in 2015.

item.title: What I Saw Crossing Into South Lebanon
item.description: Our visual journalist David Guttenfelder traveled with displaced people returning to their homes in southern Lebanon, as a cease-fire paused the fighting between Israel and Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: David Guttenfelder, Coleman Lowndes, Christina Thornell, Sutton Raphael, Joey Sendaydiego and Thomas Vollkommer

item.title: Trump Extends Cease-Fire, Even as Peace Talks Are Put on Hold
item.description: With the cease-fire on the verge of ending, President Trump said on Tuesday that he would keep it in place until Iran’s “proposal is submitted, and discussions are concluded, one way or the other.”
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Swan, Maggie Haberman, Elian Peltier and Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A mural in Tehran meant to show that Iran has a tight grip on the Strait of Hormuz. The status of the strait is one of the sticking points in negotiations between Iran and the United States.
